violations.text: Various prepped foods in the walk-in cooler were observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. PIC informed. New Violation. Correct By: 05/14/2025
The non-food contact surfaces of various equipment have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 05/28/2025

The floors (by walk-in cooler, 3 bay sink, stairs to basement, and basement) and walls (especially by walk-in cooler) throughout the facility have an accumulation of soil residue and/or food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 05/28/2025

Insufficient lighting observed in the walk-in cooler. Sufficient light is needed to enable employees to perform certain functions such as reading labels; discerning the color of substances; identifying toxic materials; recognizing the condition of food, utensils, and supplies; and safely conducting general food establishment operations and clean-up. Properly distributed light makes the need for cleaning apparent by making accumulations of soil conspicuous. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/28/2025
